prompt: Why does sizing PV, then battery, then genset independently produce a worse system?
options: Because each component has a different supplier
Because each block's size depends on the others through the chosen dispatch
Because batteries always come last
Because the simulator can't read the spreadsheet
The genset only runs when the battery is full
The genset matches load instantaneously and battery covers gaps
The genset runs at high load and tops up the battery
PV is curtailed every cycle
Cost per litre of fuel
Lifetime cost divided by lifetime energy served
Net present cost minus subsidies
Tariff approved by the regulator

prompt: Unmet load in a hybrid optimisation report means…
options: Energy generated but not sold
Customers who have not connected yet
Fraction of demand that the system never delivers
Battery capacity unused at end of day
There is exactly one optimal sizing per demand profile
Many sizings meet a target; the optimiser finds the lowest-cost
Dispatch is decided after sizing
Renewable fraction does not affect LCoE
